methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $1.5B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same-stage peers in Space & Aerospace. Range: $1.0B - $2.5B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Space & Aerospace P/S median is 59.8x across 6 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
Varda Space Industries, a Series C-stage space and aerospace company, is valued at $1.5B post-money following $131M in total capital raised. The valuation assessment draws from a comparable universe of 4 private peers at the same stage (Axiom Space, Impulse Space, Skyryse, Isar Aerospace) with valuations ranging $1.0B–$2.5B, alongside 5 public sector comps (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL).

Two quantitative methods triangulate to a defensible range. The stage-progression method applies a 1.7x step-up multiplier typical of Series C advancement, implying $2.5B. The peer-median approach anchors to the $1.5B median valuation across the 4 same-stage, same-sector comparables, which range $1.0B–$2.5B. Public-comp anchoring via P/S multiples (sector median 59.8x across 6 tickers) is not applicable due to unavailable revenue disclosure. 

Triangulating these two methods yields a valuation range of $1.3B (low) to $2.9B (high), with a midpoint of $2.0B. The current $1.5B post-money aligns with peer-median positioning but sits below the stage-progression implied value, suggesting either conservative pricing or below-median growth expectations relative to the peer set. Key uncertainties include revenue trajectory, capital intensity of manufacturing scale-up, and competitive pressure within the in-space manufacturing subsector.
